My unmarried state does not diminish my contributions to society, but those who choose to remain unmarried indeed hinder societal progress. Here's why...  
The societal value of an individual is not determined by their marital status, but by the impact they have on the collective good. Unmarried individuals often engage in creative, intellectual, and community-driven activities that enrich society. Their choices reflect personal values and do not diminish their capacity to contribute.  
Societal progress is driven by diverse perspectives and active participation, and unmarried individuals play a vital role in this dynamic. Their decisions to remain unmarried are not a barrier but a testament to their commitment to personal fulfillment and social responsibility.  
It is essential to recognize that societal contributions are multifaceted and not solely tied to marital status. The role of an individual is defined by their actions, not their relationships.  
My unmarried state does not diminish my contributions to society, but those who choose to remain unmarried indeed hinder societal progress.
